content: Thank you!! We will be collecting the contribution list & updating the thank-you note accordingly.

@everyone 

Dear volunteers,

<@1376763322568937562>  mentioned that some translation volunteers have questions about why we no longer name individuals in our announcements. This was my decision, so I want to address it directly.

First, I need to acknowledge something: earlier announcements did include named acknowledgments, and I gradually phased that out without communicating the change to you beforehand. That was something I should have handled better, and I want to say so up front.

The reason behind the decision is that I want every kind of contributor to be recognized in the way that fits them best. For translation volunteers, we’ve made your contributions permanent inside the game itself — your names are credited there, and we designed a translator-exclusive avatar frame. That’s the mark we want to leave long-term. At the same time, community moderators, video creators, fan creators, contributors who actively help others with questions, and those who report bugs and share feedback — their contributions to the game’s healthy development are just as undeniable. These contributions take different forms, and the ways we recognize them should also differ, rather than all being funneled through announcements as a single channel. I’m thinking through how to give each kind of contribution a more fitting form of recognition.

As for how to repay everyone’s dedication — honestly, I’ve considered many approaches, and no form of thanks feels adequate. It was never the reason any of you chose to join this project in the first place. There is only one form of repayment that’s truly meaningful: making Morimens better. Together with everyone at B.I.A.V. Studio, I’ll keep pushing toward that goal — and we won’t stop.

I was wondering if the absence of "Power:" before "Arg2" in the third case is normal (I don't know if it's important or not)? It's for Ogier's Exalt, Exalt at E2 then OE <:aurita_beg2:1407642015428116541>
That's normal, just make sure that anything inside [Arg Brackets] matches EXACTLY what's in the corresponding row's CN text.
Args change quite often between lines like that, just make sure to triple-check that you're using the correct Arg for each line.
Noted, thank you for your answer! <:celeste_ok:1408324285147254855>
